title: hieracium florentinum subsp. michelii
intro: Hieracium florentinum subsp. michelii, also known as Hawkweed or Michel's Hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion-like Asteraceae family, characterized by its yellow or orange daisy-like flowers and bitter leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hieracium florentinum subsp. michelii include its potential to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its use as a natural diuretic and to support liver health. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications, such as salves or creams, to harness its medicinal properties.
